questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Young's modulus is a measure of the stiffness of a solid.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is defined as the ratio of stress to strain within the proportional limit of the material.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Young's modulus is indeed a measure of the stiffness of a solid, and the given reason explains why.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Bulk modulus is a measure of the resistance offered by a solid to change in volume.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is the ratio of the change in pressure to the resulting change in volume.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Bulk modulus indeed measures the resistance to change in volume, and the given reason explains the definition.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Shear modulus is a measure of the rigidity of a solid.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is the ratio of shear stress to shear strain within the proportional limit of the material.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Shear modulus indeed measures the rigidity of a solid, and the given reason explains the definition.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Poisson's ratio is a measure of the lateral strain produced by a tensile loading.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is defined as the ratio of lateral strain to longitudinal strain.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Poisson's ratio indeed measures the lateral strain produced by a tensile loading, and the given reason explains why.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The bulk modulus of a solid is always greater than its Young's modulus.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Bulk modulus is a measure of resistance to change in volume, which is higher than resistance to change in length.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This statement is true, and the given reason explains why bulk modulus is greater than Young's modulus.
